assets.notes: Slice-1 hero + style anchor. 64px top-down pixel wolf spider, faces EAST (rotation=velocity.angle()).

assets.label: enemy-trapdoor-spider
assets.notes: Trapdoor spider enemy, styled off the wolf anchor. Faces east.

assets.label: larva
assets.notes: Wandering larva/grub. Faces east.

assets.label: web-shot
assets.notes: Silk projectile blob.

assets.label: web-trap
assets.notes: Circular web snare.

assets.label: web-shot-spent
assets.notes: Splattered web on wall impact.

assets.label: web-trap-spent
assets.notes: Torn spent web left after consumption.

assets.label: tunnel-floor-wall-15piece
assets.notes: generate_tileset, pixel/topdown/15-piece/32px/two_surfaces. Prompt: 'packed dirt tunnel floor and rough dirt-brown stone wall, floor #2B2621 wall #4F453B'. Dark blue-black floor with orange-lit cracks (volcanic/lava-cave read, not the requested dirt-brown) -- user picked it for the atmosphere anyway over 3 closer-to-palette alternatives. 4x4 atlas, 32px cells, SpriteCook dual-grid mask order.

assets.label: wall-material-v3
assets.notes: generate_game_art mode=texture, bg_mode=include, dirt-brown palette (#4F453B/#2B2621). Third attempt: first two ('pebble'/'stone' wording) produced strong vertical rock-column banding that survived a prompt tweak toward 'irregular, non-repeating'; only dropping pebble/stone language entirely ('smooth compacted dirt wall, no embedded rocks or stones') fixed it. Verified seamless via a headless Godot 4x4 tile composite, no visible repeat or banding. Intentionally plain/subtle -- pair with assets/decals/ for visual variety, not meant to carry detail alone.

assets.label: floor-material-v3-simple
assets.notes: generate_game_art mode=texture, bg_mode=include, dirt-brown palette. Replaces floor-material-v2 (asset 0657eba0, no longer referenced by any file): v2's 'claw-drag scoring' detail read as distracting scratches in-game once wired into FloorRenderer -- user asked for something simpler. This version drops all linear/scratch detail entirely ('no scratches, no claw marks, no linear details, plain simple flat surface'), same trick that fixed wall_material's banding. Verified seamless via headless Godot 3x3 tile composite, no artifacts, calm subtle mottling only.

assets.label: wet-floor-material
assets.notes: generate_game_art-style seamless texture, murky reddish-brown clay floodwater with subtle even ripples and a dull liquid sheen. Base layer for flooded tiles (WaterIngress hazard) -- sits under water_overlay_material.png. Replaces the earlier water-material asset (0c476bad, plain blue #2673BF, never wired into code). Verified seamless via headless Godot 3x3 tile composite.

assets.label: water-overlay-material
assets.notes: generate_game_art-style seamless texture, murky greenish-brown floodwater with sharp small-scale specular highlights forming a shimmering caustic pattern. Animated overlay drawn on top of wet_floor_material.png via water_overlay.gdshader (TIME-scrolled UV). Verified seamless via headless Godot 3x3 tile composite.

assets.label: decal-root-tangle
assets.notes: generate_game_art mode=assets, bg_mode=transparent. Isolated root-tangle detail for stamping onto wall_material variety (WallOverdrawMask use case). Clean cutout, no issues found.

assets.label: decal-crack-mineral-stain
assets.notes: generate_game_art mode=assets, bg_mode=transparent. Isolated crack/mineral-stain detail for stamping onto wall_material variety (WallOverdrawMask use case). Clean cutout, no issues found.

assets.label: wolf-nswe-directional-grid
assets.notes: Wolf spider, 2x2 grid (S top-left, N top-right, W bottom-left, E bottom-right), high three-quarter oblique camera matching the faux-3D tunnel walls (not flat top-down). SLICED into wolf_south/north/west.png (auto_slice_asset) and WIRED into resources/spiders/wolf.tres -> Player/Enemy via SpiderClassData.frame_for_facing(), replacing single-sprite in-engine rotation (docs/art-bible.md §2's 2026-07-21 revision). The sheet's own EAST quadrant was discarded (see wolf-west-pose-v2's own notes below for why) -- EAST is now always the west texture mirrored via Sprite2D.flip_h, never a separate file. An underside/ceiling-plane companion set was attempted (several prompt approaches) but never converged and isn't saved anywhere -- topside-only for now.

assets.label: warden-nswe-directional-grid
assets.notes: Warden (trapdoor spider), same 2x2 NSWE grid/camera convention as wolf-nswe-directional-grid. SLICED into warden_south/north/west.png and WIRED into resources/spiders/weaver.tres (Warden's kit maps to the WEAVER class enum id, confirmed via weaver.tres's #66BF73 green display_color matching Warden's art-bible color exactly). Warden's own EAST quadrant was the one class whose west/east actually read correctly as generated -- but it was still discarded in favor of the uniform flip_h-from-west approach (see wolf-west-pose-v2's notes) so every class goes through the same, permanently-consistent code path instead of Warden being a special case.

assets.label: ogre-nswe-directional-grid
assets.notes: Ogre (net-casting spider), resting pose, same 2x2 NSWE grid/camera convention as wolf-nswe-directional-grid. SLICED into ogre_south/north.png and WIRED into resources/spiders/net_caster.tres (NET_CASTER class enum id) as the default/resting art. The sheet's own WEST/EAST quadrants were discarded -- see ogre-west-pose-v2's own notes below -- replaced by a fresh single west pose, mirrored in code for EAST. The Net Hold action-pose set (below) is sliced but NOT wired, deferred as a separate follow-up since it needs to swap in only while NetHoldSkill is actively held, not replace the resting art outright.

assets.label: ogre-net-hold-nswe-directional-grid
assets.notes: Ogre, Net Hold action pose (net held taut between front legs, cribellate-silk mesh per docs/art-bible.md §5's 2026-07-21 net-language revision), same 2x2 NSWE grid/camera convention as ogre-nswe-directional-grid. A separate set from Ogre's resting pose, for the Net Hold/Shot kit action. SLICED into ogre_net_hold_south/north/west/east.png -- via a manual quadrant crop + per-quadrant alpha-bounds trim (world/maze headless script), not auto_slice_asset, since the net's deliberately holey/semi-transparent silk mesh fragmented connected-alpha detection into 9 scattered pieces instead of 4. NOT YET WIRED -- deferred until NetHoldSkill exposes an active-hold state Player/Enemy can key the swap off of.

assets.label: echo-nswe-directional-grid
assets.notes: Echo (trashline/decoy orb-weaver), same 2x2 NSWE grid/camera convention as wolf-nswe-directional-grid. SLICED into echo_south/north.png and WIRED into resources/spiders/decoy.tres (DECOY class enum id). The sheet's own WEST/EAST quadrants were discarded -- see echo-west-pose-v2's own notes below -- replaced by a fresh single west pose, mirrored in code for EAST.

assets.label: wolf-west-pose-v2
assets.notes: Replaces wolf-nswe-directional-grid's own WEST/EAST quadrants. Root cause: the original sheet's west/east poses read as 'walking backwards' regardless of which one was assigned to which direction -- confirmed by swapping the two files against each other first (no improvement), meaning the pose itself, not the file/quadrant label, was the problem. Every class but Warden had this same defect. Single-pose regeneration (reference_asset_id = the sheet's own approved SOUTH slice, explicit mid-stride walking-left gait description: near-side legs reaching forward toward travel direction, far-side legs trailing/pushing off) fixed it -- user-confirmed correct. EAST is no longer a separate generated asset for any class: SpiderClassData.should_flip_h() mirrors this same texture via Sprite2D.flip_h instead, permanently avoiding needing the model to produce two independently-consistent mirror images.

assets.label: ogre-west-pose-v2
assets.notes: Same fix and reasoning as wolf-west-pose-v2, for Ogre. reference_asset_id = ogre-nswe-directional-grid's own approved SOUTH slice.

assets.label: echo-west-pose-v2
assets.notes: Same fix and reasoning as wolf-west-pose-v2, for Echo. reference_asset_id = echo-nswe-directional-grid's own approved SOUTH slice.
